title: 40 Celcius at Fusion Festival
description: It's come to my attention that at least 3 (!) people have set up notifications for this RSS feed so shoutouts to you guys! You've earned yourselves a new newsletter and an update on what I've been up to! I write this for me but it's a bonus if my friends read it too.

Two weeks ago there was an insane 40 degrees heatwave across all of Europe and I think it was a wake-up call for everyone about the new normal when it comes to extreme weather. We're living in a time of change and it won't be long before our lives will truly and regularly be disrupted by weather like this.

I happened to have spent that weekend at Fusion festival, the largest festival I've ever been too. I decided to go because after years of FOMO and so many of my mates going this year, I thought it would be nice to check it out. I heard that it's almost like its own separate country, a fantasy land that you can retreat to for a long weekend.

Tragically, bad luck meant that we had to wait for hours to get in (there was a forest fire and an evacuation), and then it became insufferably hot. My tent was an oven, and unlike many people around me, I was simply unable to get more than 3 hours sleep, which ultimately meant that I couldn't enjoy the weekend. Without sleep I'm not able to do anything with the enthusiasm that I normally carry with me.

It's a real shame because I do recognise that it is cool there. It really does have everything. The Kino was great, and I saw this one outstanding disco band. But I also feel like some things don't need to grow this big. You never saw the same people twice, and although I knew lots of people there, I didn't get to know anyone new. I also felt overwhelmed by the choice of activities. As a sufferer of FOMO, it was really hard to decide what to do.

The next time I go to a festival, it will be a small one where I stay for maximum 2 nights, and if there is a heatwave, I will simply not go. I don't wanna be in survival mode and also pretend I'm having fun.

In website news, I've started working on an exciting new project - making music mixes! I already have a ton of mixes that I made over the years for video game music, and as preparation for the new project, I decided to make some fun album art for all of those. I totally underestimated how many I have (I made 65!! wtf!) so it took ages, but I learned a lot doing it. Graphic design really is my passion. I also found an unfinished one that I finished off!

entries.title: Her Story
description: Wow, this game really blew my mind! Fab and I played it together and loved it. It's a detective game where you have to figure out what's going on, and you do so by watching video clips from an old, bugged out database. By searching keywords you can slowly but surely extract information and piece together an insane story about this lady. Everytime there is a little revelation, it's amazing. It's just written so well!

And her acting is great. And it's British! I was worried going into this that I would have to listen to an American accent the whole time but no she's in Portsmouth, it's great. Vibes of the game are great too, it all takes place in 1994.
If you have also played this, let's have a chat about it, I wanna know what you think about the MPD theory because I am skeptical.

entries.title: Street Fighter II: The Animated Movie
description: My colleague Erik sent me a dailymotion link to this at work and it just started autoplaying in the chat. Before I knew it I was watching this whole ass film while at work. Despite just having it on in the background I found it very entertaining, although I feel like they did Chun-Li dirty, man. They didn't need to have that shower scene and her be almost naked for her fight scene. At least she won the fight with spinning bird kick!

entries.title: Metropolis
description: Decided to watch this at home on my own for my education and it was a lot better than all these other silent films I've been watching because it has a lot of substance and nice imagery. It's long but honestly, lots to enjoy.
entries.metadata: Release: 1927, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: Berlin: Symphony of a Metropolis
description: I went to this at Fusion festival thinking it would be Fritz Lang's Metropolis also released in 1927, but no it's this 'portrait of a city'-type silent film, which was honestly also great, although I admit I took a really strong gummy for the viewing. There were two DJs playing music along with the film which was honestly great and added hugely to the vibe. Really enjoyed it.

entries.title: Try it - A FLINTA* Skate Documentary
description: Watched this at Fusion festival because it was 40 degrees outside and the cinema was cool. It's about how it's tough for flinta to get into skating because its male dominated. Entertainment value was like a 3/10 but I did see some parallels with girls in the smash community. You can watch it here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cyBMTS9jkOU

entries.title: The Activist
description: Watched this for Mongay and I was on board for a little while but then there was this ridiculous workout montage and then I couldn't take it seriously any more.
entries.metadata: Release: 2025, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: F-Zero 99
description: An extension to the SNES soundtrack! It is actually super cool that they created brand new SNES music for this game, although they only really made new versions of the same old tunes. The '??? Remixes' are a bit special because they combine two tracks, so the Mute City Remix is actually a mash up of Mute City and Fire Field. It's fun, but honestly they don't make for nice listening.
entries.metadata: Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

entries.title: Rhythm Paradise Groove
description: I'm excited to play this game, but I don't know about these music tracks. They're pretty bad. But the magic comes from playing the game and tapping to the beat, I guess.
entries.metadata: Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

entries.title: Hyrule Warriors: Age of Imprisonment
description: I don't know too much about this game, but I'm told it's quite good. Listening to the soundtrack I can see that it takes you through all the locales from the main game and provides high energy interpretations of Tears of the Kingdom's music.

I learned that the team behind NieR: Automata's excellent soundtrack made this one! I think they did a pretty excellent job in recreating that BOTW vibe, I would have thought it was from the original composers. The highlights for me are the battle themes, and specifically the remixes of the various areas like Korok Forest, Zora's Domain etc, because I'm just a bitch for remixes of old, recognisable songs. But there's definitely an entire, excellent, vibes-heavy but still energetic soundtrack here for us all to enjoy.
entries.metadata: Platform: Switch 2, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

entries.title: Kirby's Dream Land 3 Mix
description: I totally slept on this soundtrack in the past, but there is some real progressive stuff in here. This is a fun little mix with the same Kirby vibe that we all know and love, but with some genuinely surprising stuff in there.
entries.metadata: For a download link, please visit the video game mixes page!
